description: The liver: the body's quiet worker, known to every tradition of medicine and every kitchen. Joins 'korua' (heart), 'kumoe' (stomach), and 'lorika' (blood) in the inner anatomy.
sound_symbolism: Low 'lo', deep 'mu', open 'ra' — a heavy, quiet, unglamorous word for a heavy, quiet, essential organ.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lomura mua weloni nai.' (The liver is in the body.) 'lomura lorika temoe.' (The liver tends the blood.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The organs that work unseen and unthanked are the body's lesson in selfless function — service without announcement.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional medicine read health in the liver long before chemistry explained why it was right to.
